bytes.topic: Zig — comptime, allocators, error unions, C interop, no hidden allocations
bytes.title: rust_sys: Taming Comptime with Zig's Allocators
bytes.body: When working with comptime in rust_sys, you can use Zig's allocators to handle memory management. Zig's allocators provide a way to allocate memory at comptime, eliminating the need for runtime allocations. To use Zig's allocators, you can import the `zig_std_allocator` crate and use the `std.heap` module. For example, you can use the `std.heap.page_allocator` to allocate memory at comptime: `let allocator = std.heap.page_allocator; let ptr = allocator.alloc(u8, 10);`. This approach ensures that memory is allocated at compile-time, reducing runtime overhead.
bytes.code_snippet: let allocator = std.heap.page_allocator; let ptr = allocator.alloc(u8, 10);
